text: How did Enlightenment philosophy lead to the French Revolution?
description: Trace the intellectual origins of 1789, from Voltaire and Rousseau to the storming of the Bastille.

text: What chain of events led from the Stamp Act to American independence?
description: Follow the escalating colonial crisis from taxation disputes to the Declaration of Independence.

text: What chain of events connected World War I to World War II?
description: Explore how the Treaty of Versailles, economic collapse, and political extremism made a second world war inevitable.

text: How did the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand escalate into a world war?
description: Unravel the alliance systems, mobilization plans, and diplomatic failures of July 1914.

text: How did the fall of Rome reshape European civilization?
description: Examine the military, economic, and cultural forces that ended Rome and birthed medieval Europe.

text: What caused the gradual decline of the Ottoman Empire?
description: Trace centuries of military setbacks, institutional rigidity, and nationalist movements that dismantled a superpower.

text: How did the Silk Road shape the modern world?
description: Follow the flow of goods, ideas, religions, and diseases along history's most famous trade network.

text: What combination of factors caused the Great Depression?
description: Analyze the interplay of speculation, bank failures, trade policy, and monetary decisions that triggered global collapse.

text: How did the Renaissance emerge from the Dark Ages?
description: Discover how trade wealth, classical rediscovery, and patronage ignited Europe's cultural rebirth.

text: How did the printing press fuel the Protestant Reformation?
description: Explore how Gutenberg's invention amplified Martin Luther's ideas and shattered religious unity in Europe.

text: How did the Seven Years' War reshape the global balance of power?
description: Examine how this first true world war redrew colonial maps and planted seeds of revolution.

text: What were the root causes of the Cold War?
description: Trace how wartime allies became superpower rivals through ideology, atomic weapons, and competing visions of world order.
